skill.name: azure-ai-document-intelligence-ts
skill.description: Extract text, tables, and structured data from documents using Azure Document Intelligence (@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence). Use when processing invoices, receipts, IDs, forms, or building custom document models.
skill.summary: Build TypeScript workflows for Azure AI Document Intelligence extraction, polling, models, and classifiers.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many findings, but review found the command execution, weak crypto, sensitive file, and critical combination detections are false positives from Markdown code fences and TypeScript examples. The confirmed risks are low and expected for this skill: examples send documents to Azure, read a local document, and use environment variables for credentials or SAS URLs. No prompt injection, obfuscated behavior, or malicious exfiltration instructions were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby and shell backtick findings are false positives. The referenced locations are Markdown fences, inline package names, and TypeScript SDK examples, not executable skill scripts or dynamic shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the flagged regions are visible code blocks or inline examples. No executable script file, shell interpolation, or dynamic command runner is present.

low_findings.title: Credential Environment Variables in Examples
low_findings.description: The skill shows environment variables for an Azure endpoint, API key, and training container SAS URL. This is expected SDK configuration, but users must avoid exposing real secrets in prompts, logs, or generated files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly reference credential-bearing environment variables. The usage is legitimate for Azure authentication and training data access, and no evidence shows secret exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Document Content Leaves the Local Environment in Examples
low_findings.description: The examples analyze documents by URL and submit local file contents to Azure Document Intelligence. This is the skill's intended function, but it can process sensitive invoices, IDs, receipts, or forms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SDK calls and local file read are explicit and normal for document analysis. The risk depends on the user's document sensitivity and Azure configuration, not on malicious skill behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Heuristics Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and critical combination findings are not supported by the file content. The base64 conversion is request payload encoding for a document file, and no obfuscated code or malicious network destination was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are ordinary SDK samples, model tables, or Azure training configuration. I found no cryptographic operation, packed payload, hidden instruction, or unauthorized destination.

content.value_statement: Document processing projects often need reliable TypeScript examples for OCR, forms, tables, and custom models. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for Azure AI Document Intelligence workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows installation for the Azure Document Intelligence REST SDK and Azure Identity.
Explains authentication with DefaultAzureCredential or an API key.
Demonstrates document analysis from a remote URL or a local file.
Lists common prebuilt models for OCR, layout, invoices, receipts, IDs, and forms.
Covers custom model building, classifier building, polling, error checks, and pagination.
limitations: Requires an Azure Document Intelligence resource and valid credentials.
Provides guidance and examples, not a complete application or wrapper library.
Does not provision Azure resources, manage pricing, or configure data retention.
Users must validate extracted fields and confidence scores for production use.

use_cases.title: Automate invoice extraction
use_cases.description: Build a TypeScript workflow that extracts vendors, totals, due dates, and line items from invoices.

use_cases.title: Add OCR to document intake
use_cases.description: Analyze uploaded PDFs or images and return text, tables, selection marks, and page structure.

use_cases.title: Train custom document models
use_cases.description: Use Azure Blob training data to build custom models or classifiers for specialized document sets.

prompt_templates.title: Start a layout analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single document URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to analyze a PDF URL with Azure AI Document Intelligence in TypeScript using the prebuilt layout model.

prompt_templates.title: Extract invoice fields
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate extraction from a business document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TypeScript example that extracts vendor, invoice total, due date, and confidence values from an invoice.

prompt_templates.title: Handle local files safely
prompt_templates.scenario: Local upload workflow with error handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me analyze a local document file with Azure Document Intelligence and include polling, error handling, and privacy notes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a custom model flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced model training and classification workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a TypeScript workflow for building a custom document model and classifier from Azure Blob training data.

output_examples.input: Analyze a receipt and summarize the important fields.
output_examples.output: A concise summary with merchant name, transaction date, total amount, item names, item prices, and confidence notes.

output_examples.input: Explain which prebuilt model to use for an ID document workflow.
output_examples.output: A recommendation to use the ID document model, with notes about supported fields, validation, and handling sensitive personal data.

output_examples.input: Plan a classifier for invoices and receipts.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step plan covering training data layout, Blob container access, classifier creation, polling, and test document evaluation.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential when possible and keep API keys out of prompts and logs.
Check unexpected responses and wait for long-running pollers before reading results.
Set confidence thresholds and human review rules for important extracted fields.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production API keys or SAS URLs into chat prompts.
Do not send regulated documents to Azure without confirming policy and retention settings.
Do not rely on extracted values without validation for financial or identity workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill run Azure calls by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides TypeScript guidance and examples for using the Azure REST SDK.

faq.question: Which authentication methods are covered?
faq.answer: It covers DefaultAzureCredential and API key authentication for Azure Document Intelligence.

faq.question: Can it process local files?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show reading a local document and submitting its encoded content to Azure.

faq.question: Does it support custom models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for building custom models and document classifiers from Blob storage.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive documents?
faq.answer: Use it only after confirming your Azure data handling, privacy, retention, and compliance requirements.

faq.question: Can it replace result validation?
faq.answer: No. You should validate extracted fields, confidence scores, and edge cases before production use.
